title: The Milkmaid and Her Pail
lines: A Milkmaid was walking to the market, carrying a pail of milk on her head.
As she went along, she began to think of what she would do with the money for the milk.
"I will buy eggs from the money I get for this milk," she said to herself.
"These eggs will hatch, and I will have many chickens."
"I will feed them, and when they are grown, I will sell them."
"With that money I will buy a new dress, the finest in all the county."
"When I wear it, all the young men will admire me, and I shall have my choice of them."
In her excitement, she tossed her head proudly, and the pail of milk fell to the ground.
Her dreams vanished with the spilled milk.
moral: Do not count your chickens before they are hatched.
